ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND Cannot find module with ESM imports
ERR_REQUIRE_ESM Must use import to load ES Module

EADDRINUSE port already in use when starting Node.js server
ERR_UNKNOWN_FILE_EXTENSION when running TypeScript files directly
body:
Node.js doesn't natively understand `.ts` files. This error occurs when trying to run TypeScript without a loader.

```
TypeError [ERR_UNKNOWN_FILE_EXTENSION]: Unknown file extension ".ts"
```

```bash
# WRONG — Node.js can't run .ts directly
node app.ts
# TypeError: Unknown file extension ".ts"

# Fix 1: Use tsx (recommended for development)
npx tsx app.ts
# Or install globally: npm install -g tsx
tsx app.ts
tsx watch app.ts  # Watch mode

# Fix 2: Use ts-node with ESM loader
npx ts-node --esm app.ts
# Or with loader flag:
node --loader ts-node/esm app.ts

# Fix 3: Node.js 22.6+ experimental strip-types
node --experimental-strip-types app.ts
# Strips type annotations, doesn't do type checking
# Does NOT support: enums, namespaces, decorators, paths

# Fix 4: Compile first
npx tsc
node dist/app.js

# For production, always compile:
# tsc → node dist/app.js
# Or use a bundler: tsup, esbuild, swc
```

```json
// tsconfig.json for Node.js 22
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "node16",
    "moduleResolution": "node16",
    "outDir": "dist"
  }
}
```

Use `tsx` for development, compile with `tsc` or `tsup` for production. Node.js 22.6+ has experimental `--experimental-strip-types` for basic TypeScript support.

Warning: --experimental-loader is deprecated, use --import with register()
body:
Node.js 22 deprecates `--loader` / `--experimental-loader` in favor of the `--import` flag with `module.register()` API.

```
(node:12345) ExperimentalWarning: `--experimental-loader` may be removed in the future;
instead use `register()`:
--import 'data:text/javascript,import { register } from "node:module"; register("ts-node/esm");'
```

```bash
# OLD (deprecated in Node.js 22)
node --loader ts-node/esm app.ts
node --experimental-loader ./custom-loader.mjs app.js

# NEW — use --import with register()
node --import 'data:text/javascript,import { register } from "node:module"; register("ts-node/esm");' app.ts

# Or create a registration file:
```

```javascript
// register.mjs
import { register } from 'node:module';
register('ts-node/esm', import.meta.url);

// Then: node --import ./register.mjs app.ts
```

```bash
# For tsx (simpler):
node --import tsx app.ts
# tsx has built-in register() support

# For custom loaders:
node --import ./register.mjs app.js
```

The `--import` flag runs a module before the main entry point. Use it with `module.register()` to install custom loaders. Most TypeScript runners (tsx) already support this.
